agents.prompt:
You are fixing non-standard CONTEXT headers in service daemon files. Many files in source/services/ have a non-standard header format like:

```
//! CONTEXT: ...
//! INTENT: ...
//! IDL (target): ...
//! DEPS: ...
//! READINESS: ...
//! TESTS: ...
```

These need to be rewritten to the STANDARD format:

```
// Copyright 2026 Open Nexus OS Contributors
// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

//! CONTEXT: [Brief description - what the module/crate does]
//! OWNERS: @runtime
//! STATUS: [Functional/Experimental/Placeholder]
//! API_STABILITY: [Stable/Unstable]
//! TEST_COVERAGE: [Number] tests or "No tests"
//! ADR: docs/adr/[relevant-adr].md
```

For test files: use test header format (TEST_SCOPE, TEST_SCENARIOS, DEPENDENCIES).

RULES:
- Descriptions in English, based on actual code, no company names (Apple, OHOS, Fuchsia, OpenHarmony)
- STATUS: check the actual code. If it's a stub/placeholder with just "print ready" → Placeholder. If real logic → Functional
- ADR must be a real file from docs/adr/ directory. Check the directory listing for available ADRs
- TEST_COVERAGE: count actual test functions, check for related test files
- For daemon files that are clearly stubs (just print "ready" and exit), mark STATUS: Placeholder

FILES TO FIX - these have non-standard INTENT/IDL/DEPS/READINESS/TESTS format that needs rewriting:

agents.result:
### SUMMARY
Successfully rewrote non-standard CONTEXT headers across ~55 source files. All old INTENT/IDL/DEPS/READINESS/TESTS format headers have been replaced with the standard format (CONTEXT, OWNERS: @runtime, STATUS, API_STABILITY, TEST_COVERAGE, ADR). Test files now use the test-specific header format (TEST_SCOPE, TEST_SCENARIOS, DEPENDENCIES). Fixed duplicate copyright notices in updated/src/lib.rs and policyd/src/std_server.rs. The workspace builds cleanly with `cargo check --workspace`.

// Copyright 2026 Open Nexus OS Contributors

<file path="docs/standards/DOCUMENTATION_STANDARDS.md" total_lines="540" shown_lines="1-100" truncated="true" next_start_line="101">
     1│ # Documentation Standards
     2│ 
     3│ ## Overview
     4│ 
     5│ This document defines the documentation standards for Open Nexus OS, including CONTEXT headers, ADR references, and CODEOWNERS structure.
     6│ 
     7│ **Related Standards**:
     8│ 
     9│ - `BUILD_STANDARDS.md` — Feature gates, `no_std` dependency hygiene, Makefile/justfile rules
    10│ - `SECURITY_STANDARDS.md` — Security invariants, threat models, testing requirements
    11│ 
    12│ ## Quick Reference
    13│ 
    14│ ### CONTEXT Header Format
    15│ 
    16│ ```rust
    17│ // Copyright 2024 Open Nexus OS Contributors
    18│ // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
    19│ 
    20│ //! CONTEXT: [Brief description]
    21│ //! OWNERS: @team-name
    22│ //! STATUS: Functional/Experimental/Placeholder/Deprecated
    23│ //! API_STABILITY: Stable/Unstable
    24│ //! TEST_COVERAGE: [Number] tests or "No tests"
    25│ //! ADR: docs/adr/[number]-[module-name]-architecture.md
    26│ ```
    27│ 
    28│ ### Status Categories
    29│ 
    30│ - **Functional**: Fully implemented and tested
    31│ - **Experimental**: Works but API may change
    32│ - **Placeholder**: Only stubs, not functional
    33│ - **Deprecated**: Will be removed/replaced
    34│ 
    35│ ### Team Responsibilities
    36│ 
    37│ - **@kernel-team**: Kernel implementation and architecture
    38│ - **@runtime**: Userspace libraries, services, and runtime components
    39│ - **@tools-team**: Build tools and development utilities
    40│ 
    41│ ## Detailed Standards
    42│ 
    43│ ### Standard Format
    44│ 
    45│ All Rust source files must include a CONTEXT header at the top with the following structure:
    46│ 
    47│ ```rust
    48│ // Copyright 2024 Open Nexus OS Contributors
    49│ // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
    50│ 
    51│ //! CONTEXT: [Brief description of module purpose]
    52│ //! OWNERS: @team-name
    53│ //! STATUS: Functional/Experimental/Placeholder/Deprecated
    54│ //! API_STABILITY: Stable/Unstable
    55│ //! TEST_COVERAGE: [Number] tests or "No tests"
    56│ //! ADR: docs/adr/[number]-[module-name]-architecture.md
    57│ ```
    58│ 
    59│ ### Header Fields
    60│ 
    61│ #### CONTEXT
    62│ 
    63│ - **Purpose**: Brief description of what the module does
    64│ - **Format**: Single line, descriptive
    65│ - **Examples**:
    66│   - "Virtual file system client library"

    74│ - **Teams**: @kernel-team, @runtime, @tools-team
    75│ 
    76│ #### STATUS
    77│ 
    78│ - **Purpose**: Indicates implementation maturity
    79│ - **Values**:
    80│   - `Functional`: Fully implemented and tested
    81│   - `Experimental`: Works but API may change
    82│   - `Placeholder`: Only stubs, not functional
    83│   - `Deprecated`: Will be removed/replaced
    84│ 
    85│ #### API_STABILITY
    86│ 
    87│ - **Purpose**: Indicates API stability for external consumers
    88│ - **Values**:
    89│   - `Stable`: API won't change without major version bump
    90│   - `Unstable`: API may change in minor versions
    91│ 
    92│ #### TEST_COVERAGE
    93│ 
    94│ - **Purpose**: Documents actual test coverage
    95│ - **Format**: "[Number] tests" or "No tests"
    96│ - **Examples**: "3 unit tests", "1 integration test", "No tests"
    97│ 
    98│ #### ADR

+//! CONTEXT: P0 (priority-zero) unit tests for dsoftbusd core modules — FSM, discovery state, handshake, typed IDs, entry helpers, and QUIC frames.
+//! OWNERS: @runtime
+//! STATUS: Functional
+//! API_STABILITY: Stable
+//! TEST_COVERAGE: 15 tests
+//!
+//! TEST_SCOPE:
+//!   - Session FSM reconnect, phase transitions, epoch tracking
+//!   - Discovery peer-IP insert/update/lookup and const defaults
+//!   - Typed netstack ID roundtrip (UdpSocketId, ListenerId, SessionId)
+//!   - Deterministic test secret derivation (cross-module)
+//!   - Cross-VM IP detection and nonce generation
+//!   - Peer-IP LRU-ordered rebuild
+//!   - QUIC frame encode/decode roundtrip and rejection (bad magic, truncated, oversized)
+//!   - QUIC opcode contract values
+//!
+//! TEST_SCENARIOS:
+//!   - test_begin_reconnect_clears_sid_and_bumps_epoch(): Reconnect clears SID and advances epoch
+//!   - test_set_phase_progression_smoke(): All SessionFsm phase setters work correctly
+//!   - test_set_peer_ip_insert_then_update(): Discovery set_peer_ip inserts and updates
+//!   - test_get_peer_ip_missing_returns_none(): Missing peer lookup returns None; consts are correct
+//!   - test_typed_id_roundtrip_raw(): UdpSocketId/ListenerId/SessionId raw roundtrip
+//!   - test_derive_test_secret_deterministic_and_distinct(): Secret derivation is deterministic and distinct per input
+//!   - test_entry_pure_is_cross_vm_ip(): Cross-VM IP detection is correct
+//!   - test_entry_pure_next_nonce_wraps(): Nonce counter wraps correctly
+//!   - test_entry_pure_rebuild_peer_ips_preserves_lru_order(): Peer IP rebuild preserves LRU order and drops stale entries
+//!   - test_entry_pure_set_get_and_derive_helpers(): Set/get peer IP and cross-module derive helpers
+//!   - test_quic_frame_roundtrip(): QUIC frame encode/decode roundtrip
+//!   - test_reject_quic_frame_bad_magic(): QUIC frame with bad magic byte is rejected
+//!   - test_reject_quic_frame_truncated_payload(): QUIC frame with truncated payload is rejected
+//!   - test_reject_quic_frame_oversized_payload_encode(): Oversized QUIC frame payload is rejected at encode time
+//!   - test_quic_frame_opcode_contract_values(): QUIC opcode constants have correct values
+//!
+//! DEPENDENCIES:

//! CONTEXT: Ability Manager daemon domain library (service API and handlers)
//! INTENT: Ability/feature lifecycle (start/stop/connect/terminate), focus/foreground mgmt, continuation
//! IDL (target): startAbility(intent), stopAbility(id), connectAbility(id), terminateAbility(id), queryAbilities(filter)
//! DEPS: samgr (resolve), bundlemgrd (manifest/required caps), dsoftbusd (continuation)
//! READINESS: print "abilitymgr: ready"; register/heartbeat with samgr
//! TESTS: start/stop loopback OK
pub fn help() -> &'static str {
    "abilitymgr manages ability lifecycle. Usage: abilitymgr [--help]"
}

pub fn execute(args: &[&str]) -> String {
    if args.contains(&"--help") {
        help().to_string()
    } else {
        "ability manager ready".to_string()
    }
}

pub fn run() {
    let owned: Vec<String> = std::env::args().skip(1).collect();
    let refs: Vec<&str> = owned.iter().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect();
    println!("{}", execute(&refs));
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::{execute, help};

    #[test]
    fn help_message() {
        assert!(help().contains("abilitymgr"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn execute_help() {
        let output = execute(&["--help"]);
        assert!(output.contains("Usage"));
    }
}

   341│     let message =
   342│         serialize::read_message(&mut cursor, ReaderOptions::new()).context("read manifest")?;
   343│     let src = message.get_root::<bundle_manifest::Reader<'_>>().context("manifest root")?;
   344│     let mut out_builder = Builder::new_default();
   345│     {
   346│         let mut dst = out_builder.init_root::<bundle_manifest::Builder<'_>>();
   347│         dst.set_schema_version(src.get_schema_version());
   348│         dst.set_name(src.get_name().context("name")?.to_str().context("name utf8")?);
   349│         dst.set_semver(src.get_semver().context("semver")?.to_str().context("semver utf8")?);
   350│         dst.set_min_sdk(src.get_min_sdk().context("minSdk")?.to_str().context("minSdk utf8")?);
   351│         dst.set_publisher(src.get_publisher().context("publisher")?);
   352│         dst.set_signature(src.get_signature().context("signature")?);
   353│         dst.set_payload_digest(&hex::decode(sha256_hex(payload)).context("payload digest decode")?);
   354│         dst.set_payload_size(payload.len() as u64);
   355│         if let Some(sbom_bytes) = sbom {
   356│             dst.set_sbom_digest(
   357│                 &hex::decode(sha256_hex(sbom_bytes)).context("sbom digest decode")?,
   358│             );
   359│         } else {
   360│             dst.set_sbom_digest(&[]);
   361│         }
   362│         if let Some(repro_bytes) = repro_json {
   363│             dst.set_repro_digest(
   364│                 &hex::decode(sha256_hex(repro_bytes)).context("repro digest decode")?,
   365│             );
   366│         } else {
   367│             dst.set_repro_digest(&[]);
   368│         }
   369│         let src_abilities = src.get_abilities().context("abilities")?;
   370│         let mut dst_abilities = dst.reborrow().init_abilities(src_abilities.len());
   371│         for idx in 0..src_abilities.len() {
   372│             dst_abilities.set(idx, src_abilities.get(idx).context("ability entry")?);
   373│         }
   374│         let src_caps = src.get_capabilities().context("caps")?;
   375│         let mut dst_caps = dst.reborrow().init_capabilities(src_caps.len());
   376│         for idx in 0..src_caps.len() {
   377│             dst_caps.set(idx, src_caps.get(idx).context("cap entry")?);
   378│         }
   379│     }
   380│     let mut out = Vec::new();
   381│     serialize::write_message(&mut out, &out_builder).context("serialize manifest with digests")?;
   382│     Ok(out)
   383│ }
   384│ 
   385│ #[cfg(test)]
   386│ fn sha256_hex(bytes: &[u8]) -> String {
   387│     let mut hasher = Sha256::new();
   388│     hasher.update(bytes);
   389│     hex::encode(hasher.finalize())
   390│ }
   391│ 
   392│ #[cfg(test)]
   393│ fn encode_frame(opcode: u8, message: &Builder<HeapAllocator>) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
   394│     let mut payload = Vec::new();
   395│     serialize::write_message(&mut payload, message).context("encode message")?;
   396│     let mut frame = Vec::with_capacity(1 + payload.len());
   397│     frame.push(opcode);
   398│     frame.extend_from_slice(&payload);
   399│     Ok(frame)
   400│ }
   401│ 
   402│ #[cfg(test)]
   403│ fn decode_response(frame: &[u8]) -> Result<(bool, Vec<String>)> {
   404│     let (opcode, payload) = frame.split_first().context("missing opcode")?;
   405│     if *opcode != POLICY_OPCODE_CHECK {
   406│         anyhow::bail!("unexpected opcode {opcode}");
   407│     }
   408│     let mut cursor = Cursor::new(payload);
   409│     let message =
   410│         serialize::read_message(&mut cursor, ReaderOptions::new()).context("decode message")?;
   411│     let response =
   412│         message.get_root::<check_response::Reader<'_>>().context("check response root")?;
   413│     let allowed = response.get_allowed();
   414│     let mut missing = Vec::new();
   415│     if let Ok(list) = response.get_missing() {
   416│         for idx in 0..list.len() {
   417│             let text = list.get(idx).context("missing entry")?.to_str().context("missing utf8")?;
   418│             missing.push(text.to_string());
   419│         }
   420│     }
   421│     Ok((allowed, missing))
   422│ }

     1│ //! Host-first deterministic tests for OS networking integration logic.
     2│ //!
     3│ //! NOTE: These tests intentionally avoid QEMU and MMIO. They validate pure logic that should
     4│ //! remain stable even when virtio backends or QEMU versions change.
     5│ //!
     6│ //! IMPORTANT: We intentionally do **not** depend on smoltcp's internal DHCP wire/config structs
     7│ //! here. smoltcp's DHCP config types include borrowed packet references and other details that
     8│ //! are not relevant to our OS integration logic and tend to shift across versions. Our goal is:
     9│ //! - deterministically detect "new lease vs same lease"
    10│ //! - track configured vs deconfigured
    11│ //! - produce a stable marker summary type
    12│ 
    13│ /// Minimal DHCP integration state machine:
    14│ /// - Detect "new" vs "same" config
    15│ /// - Track current lease
    16│ /// - Provide a stable `DhcpConfig` summary for marker emission
    17│ ///
    18│ /// This is a host-testable mirror of the behavior used by `SmoltcpVirtioNetStack::dhcp_poll()`.
    19│ #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
    20│ struct DhcpState {
    21│     bound_ip: Option<([u8; 4], u8)>,
    22│     bound_gateway: Option<[u8; 4]>,
    23│ }
    24│ 
    25│ #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
    26│ struct DhcpConfig {

    28│     prefix_len: u8,
    29│     gateway: Option<[u8; 4]>,
    30│ }
    31│ 
    32│ #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
    33│ enum DhcpUpdate {

    35│     Deconfigured,
    36│ }
    37│ 
    38│ #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
    39│ enum Event {
    40│     Configured { ip: [u8; 4], prefix_len: u8, gateway: Option<[u8; 4]> },
    41│     Deconfigured,
    42│ }
    43│ 
    44│ // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
    45│ // On-wire invariants (host-first)
    46│ // -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

    48│ #[test]
    49│ fn dhcp_discover_uses_standard_ports_and_broadcast() {
    50│     use smoltcp::iface::{Config as IfaceConfig, Interface, SocketSet};
    51│     use smoltcp::phy::{Device, DeviceCapabilities, Medium, RxToken, TxToken};
    52│     use smoltcp::socket::dhcpv4;

    55│         EthernetAddress, EthernetFrame, HardwareAddress, IpProtocol, Ipv4Address, Ipv4Packet,
    56│         UdpPacket,
    57│     };
    58│ 
    59│     struct CaptureDevice {
    60│         caps: DeviceCapabilities,
    61│         tx: std::vec::Vec<std::vec::Vec<u8>>,
    62│     }
    63│ 
    64│     struct CaptTx<'a> {
    65│         dev: &'a mut CaptureDevice,
    66│     }
    67│ 
    68│     impl TxToken for CaptTx<'_> {
    69│         fn consume<R, F>(self, len: usize, f: F) -> R
    70│         where
    71│             F: FnOnce(&mut [u8]) -> R,
    72│         {
    73│             let mut buf = vec![0u8; len];
    74│             let r = f(&mut buf);
    75│             self.dev.tx.push(buf);
    76│             r
    77│         }
    78│     }
    79│ 
    80│     struct NoRx;
    81│     impl RxToken for NoRx {
    82│         fn consume<R, F>(self, _f: F) -> R
    83│         where

    85│         {
    86│             unreachable!("no RX in this test")
    87│         }
    88│     }

    90│     impl Device for CaptureDevice {

    92│             = NoRx
    93│         where
    94│             Self: 'a;
    95│         type TxToken<'a>
    96│             = CaptTx<'a>
    97│         where

   122│         },

   124│     };
   125│ 
   126│     let mac = EthernetAddress([0x02, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x01]);
   127│     let hw = HardwareAddress::Ethernet(mac);
   128│     let mut cfg = IfaceConfig::new(hw);
   129│     cfg.random_seed = 0x1234_5678;
   130│     let mut iface = Interface::new(cfg, &mut dev, Instant::from_millis(0));
   131│ 
   132│     let mut sockets = SocketSet::new(std::vec::Vec::new());
   133│     let dhcp = dhcpv4::Socket::new();
   134│     let dh = sockets.add(dhcp);
   135│ 
   136│     // Drive the DHCP socket similarly to the OS loop:
   137│     // - poll interface
   138│     // - poll dhcp socket (state machine)
   139│     // - poll interface again to flush outgoing packets
   140│     for t in 0..20u64 {
   141│         let now = Instant::from_millis((t * 50) as i64);
   142│         let _ = iface.poll(now, &mut dev, &mut sockets);
   143│         let _ = sockets.get_mut::<dhcpv4::Socket>(dh).poll();
   144│         let _ = iface.poll(now, &mut dev, &mut sockets);
   145│     }
   146│ 
   147│     // Assert at least one outbound UDP frame uses DHCP client/server ports.
   148│     let mut saw = false;
   149│     for frame in &dev.tx {
   150│         let Ok(eth) = EthernetFrame::new_checked(frame) else {
   151│             continue;
   152│         };
   153│         if eth.ethertype() != smoltcp::wire::EthernetProtocol::Ipv4 {
   154│             continue;
   155│         }
   156│         let Ok(ip) = Ipv4Packet::new_checked(eth.payload()) else {
   157│             continue;
   158│         };
   159│         if ip.next_header() != IpProtocol::Udp {
   160│             continue;
   161│         }
   162│         let Ok(udp) = UdpPacket::new_checked(ip.payload()) else {
   163│             continue;
   164│         };
   165│         let src = udp.src_port();
   166│         let dst = udp.dst_port();
   167│         if src == 68 && dst == 67 {
   168│             saw = true;
   169│             // RFC 2131 discover/request originate from 0.0.0.0 and broadcast to 255.255.255.255.
   170│             let s = ip.src_addr();
   171│             let d = ip.dst_addr();
   172│             assert_eq!(s, Ipv4Address::new(0, 0, 0, 0));
   173│             assert_eq!(d, Ipv4Address::new(255, 255, 255, 255));
   174│             break;
   175│         }
   176│     }
   177│     assert!(saw, "no DHCP UDP frame (68->67) captured");
   178│ }
   179│ 
   180│ impl DhcpState {
   181│     fn handle_event(&mut self, ev: Event) -> Option<DhcpUpdate> {
   182│         match ev {
   183│             Event::Configured { ip, prefix_len, gateway } => {
   184│                 let is_new =
   185│                     self.bound_ip != Some((ip, prefix_len)) || self.bound_gateway != gateway;
   186│                 if !is_new {
   187│                     return None;
   188│                 }
   189│                 self.bound_ip = Some((ip, prefix_len));
   190│                 self.bound_gateway = gateway;
   191│                 Some(DhcpUpdate::Configured(DhcpConfig { ip, prefix_len, gateway }))
   192│             }
   193│             Event::Deconfigured => {
   194│                 self.bound_ip = None;
   195│                 self.bound_gateway = None;
   196│                 Some(DhcpUpdate::Deconfigured)
   197│             }
   198│         }
   199│     }
   200│ }

content.text:
All 10 files verified with correct headers. Let me provide the structured report.

### SUMMARY
Added standard file headers (copyright, SPDX, CONTEXT, OWNERS, STATUS, API_STABILITY, TEST_COVERAGE/TEST_SCOPE/TEST_SCENARIOS, ADR) to all 10 specified source files. Each header was crafted from the actual code content, with ADR references selected from the 30 available ADRs in `docs/adr/`. Test files received the extended test header format with TEST_SCOPE and TEST_SCENARIOS.

### EVIDENCE
- `docs/adr/` — directory listing showed 30 ADR files (0001–0031), used to select relevant ADRs for each file
- `source/drivers/net/virtio/tests/basic.rs:1-80` — 1 test function, driver integration test
- `source/services/execd/src/os_lite.rs:1-856` — no `#[test]` or `mod tests`, 856-line os-lite execd backend
- `source/services/vfsd/src/os_lite.rs:1-278` — no tests, 278-line os-lite vfsd backend
- `source/services/vfsd/src/sandbox.rs:1-279` — 5 tests in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (lines 228-279)
- `source/services/vfsd/src/std_server.rs:1-777` — 1 test in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (line 763)
- `source/services/debugsvc/src/main.rs:1-54` — no tests, minimal debug service
- `userspace/nexus-net-os/src/host_tests.rs:1-257` — 5 tests (4 DHCP state + 1 on-wire), existing doc comments preserved
- `userspace/ui/svg/tests/cursor_svg_test.rs:1-54` — 1 test function with 5 sub-scenarios
- `userspace/ui/layout/src/engine_tests.rs:1-281` — 9 tests in `mod tests`
- `tests/remote_e2e/tests/remote_pkgfs.rs:1-84` — 1 test with multiple assertions

### CHANGES
- `source/drivers/net/virtio/tests/basic.rs` — prepended standard test header (virtio MMIO driver, ADR 0018)
- `source/services/execd/src/os_lite.rs` — prepended standard header (execd os-lite backend, ADR 0007)
- `source/services/vfsd/src/os_lite.rs` — prepended standard header (vfsd os-lite backend, ADR 0017)
- `source/services/vfsd/src/sandbox.rs` — prepended standard header (sandbox/CapFdToken, ADR 0014)
- `source/services/vfsd/src/std_server.rs` — prepended standard header (vfsd Cap'n Proto server, ADR 0004)
- `source/services/debugsvc/src/main.rs` — prepended standard header (debug service, ADR 0017)
- `userspace/nexus-net-os/src/host_tests.rs` — prepended test header, preserved existing `//!` doc block (DHCP state machine, ADR 0026)
- `userspace/ui/svg/tests/cursor_svg_test.rs` — prepended test header (SVG rendering, ADR 0028)
- `userspace/ui/layout/src/engine_tests.rs` — prepended test header (layout engine, ADR 0030)
- `tests/remote_e2e/tests/remote_pkgfs.rs` — prepended test header (remote packagefs E2E, ADR 0009)

### RISKS
- None observed. Headers are purely additive (prepended before existing content); existing `//!` doc comments in `host_tests.rs` were preserved after the new header.

### BLOCKERS
None.
